what_it_does: It converges Tim's foundational theorem into a single v2 frame, marking every reconciliation and seam as a candidate for Tim to adjudicate, and maps the theorem onto the company's engine, resolver, and proposed cube instrument.
observations: v2 rewrite by fork, superseding v1, with an explicit register of candidacy and Tim as the sole judge.
Heavy provenance caveat: much geometric/theorem material is AI-projection attributed to Tim, not verbatim, so the document is one layer removed from the bedrock.
Tim-confirmed three-way separation: coordinate axes are a swappable loadout; type is n inside the equation, not an axis; instance is relativity and frame dissolves into it.
The Engine (2026-05-13) is treated as the spine, with its eight parts read as the company's architecture.
The cube is corrected from 'fails the equation' to 'the same object at k=3, behaving differently under variables'.
A consolidated open-seams registry (S1-S11 plus instrument and law seams) is presented for Tim's ratification.
The resolver `resolve(invariant, coordinate)` is offered as the theorem's formal floor already operational.
standouts: The §1 pivot: the rule declares which axes it needs, making the axis-set itself a variable.
Tim's 2026-06-20 confirmation that axes are swappable, type=n is inside the equation, and frame dissolves into instance.
The cube correction: d=2 and d=3 are regimes of one object, not pass/fail.
The candidate claim that the company's resolver already is the theorem operationalized.
conventions: Explicit status tagging: '(candidate — Tim adjudicates)' attached to reconciliations.
Provenance honesty: distinguishes verbatim, raw shards, live sessions, and AI-projected material.
Upstream/bedrock metaphor: this document is downstream of Tim's literal words.
Bracketed cross-references to sibling files (WIDEN-FINDINGS, THEOREM-SOURCES).
concerns: Builds heavily on AI-projected material while simultaneously warning that layer is not authoritative.
Many central claims are explicitly held open, so the document cannot be used as a finished specification.
Dense, specialized vocabulary may obscure actionable engineering decisions.
Risk of overclaim in reconcile sections despite the candidacy register.
Unresolved internal contradictions (axis counts, 4th root identity) are catalogued but not resolved.
notes: A self-aware convergence map that treats itself as a candidate triangulation and routes every unresolved seam back to Tim.
questions: What is the precise derivation and meaning of the seed equation `2π/n = (1/n)^k`?
Which concrete files contain the verbatim bedrock it points to?
How does `resolve(invariant, coordinate)` map to existing code paths?
What is the current implementation status of the cube/instrument and the 3-D fork?
How does the '3/1 in all directions' recursion generate the axis catalogue?
Is the four-root set the same as the four points of the parametric box?
What distinguishes the 12 substrate predicates from the 7 verbs?
What would make a seam move from candidate to confirmed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is a convergence frame and candidacy registry, and it is exactly that, plus a build-prep mapping to engine/resolver/instrument.
apparent_themes: Provenance honesty as a load-bearing practice.
Convergence between fragments as the signal, not any single fragment.
Everything is a variable, including the axis-set.
Rendering as consequence of substrate state.
Tim as the invariant judge over a collective projection.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a shared, honest baseline for Tim's theorem so the next build step can proceed without overclaiming, while keeping every unresolved question explicitly routed to Tim.
novelty: A foundational convergence document that systematically refuses authority, labels its own sources as possibly erroneous AI projections, and treats the founder's adjudication as the only valid closure mechanism.
contribution: A provenance-aware triangulation of theorem fragments, an explicit registry of open seams and their owners, and a mapping between abstract theorem concepts and concrete company components that a fused system can treat as a question-set rather than a canon.
summary_for_embedding: v2 convergence document for Tim's foundational theorem, candidately mapping axes, invariant law, geometry, instrument, verbs, and resolver while marking all seams for Tim's adjudication.
intention_for_embedding: To assemble scattered theorem fragments into one honest frame and identify what still requires Tim's ratification before building the next instrument.
